root_cause.text: The manual initialization of the trust root index by loading and indexing all trusted CA certificates causes a significant performance overhead, leading to substantial app startup delays on Android.
root_cause.evidence: The commit message notes "This results in a substantial savings in app startup - 500 milliseconds or more", and the patch introduces `AndroidTrustRootIndex` which uses reflection to directly invoke Conscrypt's `findTrustAnchorByIssuerAndSignature` method instead of building the index manually.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The manual initialization of the trust root index by loading and indexing all trusted CA certificates causes a significant performance overhead, leading to substantial app startup delays on Android.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The commit message notes "This results in a substantial savings in app startup - 500 milliseconds or more", and the patch introduces `AndroidTrustRootIndex` which uses reflection to directly invoke Conscrypt's `findTrustAnchorByIssuerAndSignature` method instead of building the index manually.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update tests to reflect the renaming of CertificateAuthorityCouncil to CertificateChainCleaner and related API changes.
intent_record.what: Renamed CertificateAuthorityCouncil to CertificateChainCleaner, updated the corresponding test class name, and changed the API calls from normalizeCertificateChain to clean while wrapping constructor arguments in RealTrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the test suite to use the newly refactored CertificateChainCleaner and RealTrustRootIndex classes instead of the deprecated CertificateAuthorityCouncil, ensuring the tests match the current API without altering the underlying test logic.

intent_record.how: Updates the test case to use the newly refactored class and method names for certificate chain cleaning.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update TLS internal imports to reflect the restructuring of certificate handling classes.

intent_record.how: Updates the class dependencies to use the newly introduced TLS certificate handling classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor CertificatePinner to use TrustRootIndex instead of CertificateAuthorityCouncil.
intent_record.what: Replaced the certificateAuthorityCouncil field with trustRootIndex in the CertificatePinner class.
intent_record.how: Updates the internal dependency used by the CertificatePinner, shifting from a CertificateAuthorityCouncil to a TrustRootIndex.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update certificate chain normalization to use CertificateChainCleaner.
intent_record.what: Replaced the usage of certificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and trustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the certificate chain normalization process to use the new CertificateChainCleaner implementation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor CertificateAuthorityCouncil to TrustRootIndex and expose its setter in the Builder.
intent_record.what: Renames internal field and setter from CertificateAuthorityCouncil to TrustRootIndex, and makes the setter public.
intent_record.how: Updates internal dependencies to use the new TrustRootIndex type and exposes the setter for external use.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update internal TLS imports from CertificateAuthorityCouncil to TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.what: Replaced the import statement for CertificateAuthorityCouncil with TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the class dependencies to reflect the renaming or restructuring of the internal TLS trust management classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace the certificate authority council field with a trust root index
intent_record.what: Replaced the CertificateAuthorityCouncil field with TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the internal representation of trusted certificate roots within the client.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor trust root index resolution to use a platform-specific implementation.
intent_record.what: Renamed certificateAuthorityCouncil to trustRootIndex and updated its instantiation to use Platform.get().trustRootIndex(trustManager).
intent_record.how: Modifies the OkHttpClient initialization to use a platform-specific trustRootIndex instead of directly creating a CertificateAuthorityCouncil from accepted issuers.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor certificate trust management by replacing CertificateAuthorityCouncil with TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.what: Replaced the CertificateAuthorityCouncil field with TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the internal component used for certificate trust management to a new type.

intent_record.intent_summary: Rename certificateAuthorityCouncil to trustRootIndex in the client builder.
intent_record.what: Changed field assignment from certificateAuthorityCouncil to trustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the internal state synchronization to use the newly named variable.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update the builder method to clear trustRootIndex instead of the obsolete certificateAuthorityCouncil field.
intent_record.what: Changed the field being reset from certificateAuthorityCouncil to trustRootIndex in the sslSocketFactory builder method.
intent_record.how: Ensures the correct internal trust management field is cleared when a custom SSLSocketFactory is provided.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for TLS TrustRootIndex classes.
intent_record.what: Added imports for AndroidTrustRootIndex, RealTrustRootIndex, and TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Makes these TLS-related classes available in the current file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add method to instantiate a TrustRootIndex from a trust manager.
intent_record.what: Added a new trustRootIndex method to the Platform class.
intent_record.how: Allows creation of a RealTrustRootIndex using the accepted issuers from an X509TrustManager.

intent_record.intent_summary: Override trustRootIndex to utilize AndroidTrustRootIndex when available on the platform.
intent_record.what: Added an overridden trustRootIndex method.
intent_record.how: Attempts to use AndroidTrustRootIndex and falls back to the superclass implementation if null.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add AndroidTrustRootIndex to optimize trust root lookups on Android using reflection.
intent_record.what: Added a new class AndroidTrustRootIndex that implements TrustRootIndex and uses reflection to call Android's internal findTrustAnchorByIssuerAndSignature method on the X509TrustManager.
intent_record.how: Improves initialization performance on Android by delegating trust root lookups directly to the OS's internal implementation, avoiding the need to manually load and index all trusted CA certificates.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor certificate chain cleaning by renaming the class and delegating trust root indexing to TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.what: The CertificateAuthorityCouncil class was renamed to CertificateChainCleaner, its internal map of certificates was replaced with a TrustRootIndex dependency, and the normalizeCertificateChain method was renamed to clean.
intent_record.how: The class now delegates the responsibility of indexing and looking up trusted root certificates to TrustRootIndex, simplifying its own state and focusing solely on cleaning the certificate chain.

intent_record.intent_summary: Delegate trusted certificate lookup and verification to the trustRootIndex component.
intent_record.what: Replaced the local findByIssuerAndSignature method call with a call to trustRootIndex.findByIssuerAndSignature and removed the explicit verifySignature check.
intent_record.how: Delegates the responsibility of finding and implicitly verifying the trusted root certificate to the trustRootIndex object, simplifying the conditional logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused findByIssuerAndSignature method to clean up the code base.
intent_record.what: Removed the findByIssuerAndSignature method from the class.
intent_record.how: Eliminates dead code that previously looked up a CA certificate by issuer and verified its signature.

intent_record.intent_summary: Introduce RealTrustRootIndex for efficient lookup and verification of trusted root certificates.
intent_record.what: Added a new class RealTrustRootIndex to map subjects to CA certificates and verify certificates by issuer and signature.
intent_record.how: Provides efficient lookup and verification of trusted root certificates using a pre-computed LinkedHashMap of subjects to certificates.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add TrustRootIndex interface to abstract trusted CA certificate lookups.
intent_record.what: Added a new TrustRootIndex interface with a single method findByIssuerAndSignature.
intent_record.how: Provides an abstraction for finding a trusted CA certificate that signed a given certificate.

grouping_result.intent: Delegate trusted certificate lookup and verification to the trustRootIndex component.
Update certificate chain normalization to use CertificateChainCleaner.
Refactor trust root index resolution to use a platform-specific implementation.
Add TrustRootIndex interface to abstract trusted CA certificate lookups.
Remove unused findByIssuerAndSignature method to clean up the code base.
Introduce RealTrustRootIndex for efficient lookup and verification of trusted root certificates.

consistency_review.core_intent: Refactor TLS certificate handling by replacing CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and TrustRootIndex.
Introduce the TrustRootIndex interface and its Android-specific implementation to optimize trusted CA certificate lookups.

relevance_review.reason: The group refactors certificate handling to introduce TrustRootIndex, which provides the necessary abstraction to avoid the manual initialization overhead of trusted CA certificates on platforms like Android.

Delegating certificate chain normalization to CertificateChainCleaner likely removes the need for manual initialization of the trust root index, directly addressing the performance overhead described in the root cause.

By changing the trust root index resolution to use a platform-specific implementation, this group directly addresses the performance overhead caused by the manual initialization of the trust root index.

The group introduces the TrustRootIndex interface and an Android-specific implementation to optimize trusted CA certificate lookups, which directly addresses the performance overhead during app startup described in the root cause.
The group only removes an unused method for code cleanup, which does not address the performance overhead caused by loading and indexing trusted CA certificates.

The group introduces a new, more efficient trust root index (RealTrustRootIndex), directly addressing the performance overhead and startup delays caused by the manual initialization of the previous index mentioned in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line retrieves all accepted issuers from the trust manager, which can be a large array on Android, and passes them to CertificateAuthorityCouncil for manual indexing, causing the performance overhead.
This is the constructor that manually loads and indexes all provided CA certificates, causing the startup delay.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement initializes the CertificateAuthorityCouncil by retrieving all accepted issuers from the trust manager, which leads to the manual indexing of all trusted CA certificates and causes a significant performance overhead during app startup.
This constructor is responsible for manually loading and indexing all provided CA certificates, which directly contributes to the reported startup delay.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Start using CertificateAuthorityCouncil in CertificatePinner.

The wiring is definitely a bit more awkward than I would
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e121ed1ad291cbe04de989683d23f549d8e305db
Author: jwilson <jwilson@squareup.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 1 20:02:07 2016 -0500

    Start using CertificateAuthorityCouncil in CertificatePinner.
    
    The wiring is definitely a bit more awkward than I would like. The
    CertificateAuthorityCountil is a non-public class, and this change
    sneaks an instance into the CertificatePinner that couples it to
    the OkHttp client's SSLSocketFactory.
    
    A nicer solution is to expose Certificate
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the vulnerability by instantiating `CertificateAuthorityCouncil` with `trustManager.getAcceptedIssuers()`, which loads and indexes all trusted CA certificates, causing the performance overhead.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Teach OkHttp to lookup the X509TrustManagerFactory.

Right now there's problems with our ability to confirm certificate
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit a7d3396047a75183398f339310774ee415c1c4e8
Author: jwilson <jwilson@squareup.com>
Date:   Wed Jan 27 00:31:15 2016 -0500

    Teach OkHttp to lookup the X509TrustManagerFactory.
    
    Right now there's problems with our ability to confirm certificate pins with
    root certificates because we don't actually know which certificate authority
    was used.
    
    This unblocks that work.
    
    This code is particularly fragile and gross. There's no API to get the
    TrustManager from
bic_trace_step.reason: The provided diff only shows the OkHttpClient constructor extracting an X509TrustManager from the SSLSocketFactory. It does not contain the vulnerable manual initialization of the trust root index or the loading of all trusted CA certificates mentioned in the root cause.
